data.title: Ocean Biomedical receives Nasdaq’s delisting letter
data.description:
• Ocean Biomedical (NASDAQ:OCEA) said on Tuesday that it has received a letter from Nasdaq, stating that the company did not comply with Nasdaq’s filing requirements because it had not filed its Form 10-K.
  • Based on its review and the materials submitted on June 17, 2024 and August 27, 2024, staff granted the company an exception until October 14, 2024, to regain compliance with the rule.
  • Upon further review, staff determined that the company did not meet the terms of the exception. Specifically, the company has not filed either its Form 10-K or Forms 10-Q as required by the rule.
  • The Company will appeal Staff’s determination to a Hearings Panel.

data.title: Disney reportedly not letting new users sign up via Apple's App Store
data.description:
Media conglomerate Disney (NYSE:DIS) is no longer allowing new customers sign up for Hulu or Disney+ via Apple's App Store, according to a Business Insider report from Tuesday.

Disney is now telling its would-be customers to pay for subscriptions on their own site instead on Apple's App Store. However, people who've already started paying for either service via Apple can keep doing that, the report said

Apple takes up to 15% of the monthly fees from customers who sign up for the subscription services and Disney CEO Bob Iger back in May expressed he doesn't want to pay that anymore and wants to change the way his company looks at distribution.

data.title: Portillo's shares could at least double in two years, activist Engaged Capital says
data.description:
Hot dog chain Portillo's (NASDAQ:PTLO) could see it shares at least double in two years with some improvements, activist Engaged Capital said on Tuesday,

Despite Portillo's having food at a great price and industry-leading average unit volumes, the restaurant operator trades at a substantial discount to growth peers, Engaged Capital highlighted in a slide deck it presented Tuesday at the 13D Active-Passive Investor Summit in New York.

Engaged argues that through a combination of improved same stores sales and revised development strategy that prioritizes on cash returns, Portillo’s current valuation multiple has the potential to re-rate from under 12x today into the high teens.

"We believe increased marketing investment, the current rollout of ordering kiosks, and a loyalty program will drive improvement in same restaurant sales," Engaged wrote in the deck. "We believe Portillo’s current valuation gives no credit for optimization of unit economic model or long duration unit growth, creating a highly attractive opportunity for current investors."

Engaged sees a scenario where Portillo's (PTLO) shares could rise to ~$26 or as high as ~$33 by December 2026.

The Engaged presentation comes after CNBC reported in August that Engaged had accumulated a nearly 10% stake and was pushing for changes. The activist is talking with management on improving operations, margins and branding as the Chicago-based chain is expanding nationally.

data.title: Seagate slips despite earnings, guidance surpassing estimates
data.description:
Seagate Technology (NASDAQ:STX) slipped 2% during early post-market trading Tuesday despite first quarter fiscal 2025 financial results and outlook surpassing estimates across the board.

Seagate reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.58, which was well above the consensus estimate of $1.46. Revenue for the quarter ended September 27 was $2.17B, versus the estimate of $2.13B. Seagate's current fiscal year runs from July 2024 through June 2025.

For the quarter in progress, Seagate forecasts adjusted earnings per share ranging from $1.65 to $2.05. With a midpoint of $1.85, it is well above the consensus estimate of $1.70. Seagate expects revenue ranging from $2.15B to $2.45B with a midpoint of $2.3B, which is again above the consensus of $2.28B.

The Fremont, Calif.-based data storage company also increased its quarterly cash dividend by 3% to $0.72 per share.

"Seagate is off to an outstanding start to the fiscal year, highlighted by gross margin expanding to the highest level in more than a decade," said CEO Dave Mosley.

"Our confidence in Seagate's future opportunities is reflected in the decision to raise the quarterly dividend as announced today," he added.

Seagate's gross margin of 32.9% for the first quarter was substantially stronger than the 10% reported for the same quarter last year.

What's more, Seagate reported a net income of $305M for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 versus a loss of $184M last year.
